meaning: n.<澳俚>诚实的,可靠的,公平的
examples: If you're dinkum, I'll help you.
It's a fair dinkum Aussie wedding.
They were asking a lot for the car, but fair dinkum considering how new it is.
Owens has a team of deputies, boosted by the addition of Dinkum (Johnny Knoxville), whose barn houses a museum of lethal hardware.
Johnny Knoxville gets unaccountably prominent billing for little more than an extended cameo as a guy named Dinkum, a screwy gun nut with a penchant for cavalier headgear.
